criteriaResults.description: Test-Path harness/orchestrate.ps1 returns true
criteriaResults.evidence: powershell Test-Path returned 'EXISTS'.

criteriaResults.description: [Parser]::ParseFile returns zero errors
criteriaResults.evidence: Ran [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile against the file -- zero errors reported (PARSE_OK).

criteriaResults.description: First three non-blank lines are #Requires -Version 5.1, Set-StrictMode -Version 3.0, $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
criteriaResults.evidence: Lines 1-3 of harness/orchestrate.ps1 are exactly '#Requires -Version 5.1', "Set-StrictMode -Version 3.0", "$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'".

criteriaResults.description: Dot-sources lib/utils.ps1, lib/invoke.ps1, lib/git.ps1, lib/planner.ps1, lib/contract.ps1, lib/generator.ps1, lib/evaluator.ps1 anchored to $PSScriptRoot in same order as orchestrate.sh
criteriaResults.evidence: harness/orchestrate.ps1:44-50 dot-source the seven libs in the order utils, invoke, git, planner, contract, generator, evaluator. orchestrate.sh:47-53 sources the .sh equivalents in the same order. Each path is built with (Join-Path $PSScriptRoot 'lib/<name>.ps1').

criteriaResults.description: AST scan finds zero forbidden POSIX tools (bash|sh|wsl|sed|awk|grep|find|realpath|mktemp|dirname|basename|tr|cut|xargs)
criteriaResults.evidence: Walked all CommandAst nodes via [Parser]::ParseFile and checked GetCommandName() against the forbidden list. Result: FORBIDDEN_NONE. Allowed externals (jq, git, gh, claude) appear via & invocation as expected.

criteriaResults.description: CLI parity: every flag accepted by orchestrate.sh is accepted by orchestrate.ps1 with the same semantics; .ps1 only adds --help/-h which the contract requires
criteriaResults.evidence: Mapped orchestrate.sh:71-127 case branches against orchestrate.ps1:111-181 switch branches. All matched: --extend, --fix, --refactor (each value-taking), --regression, --resume (flags), --project-type, --context-strategy, --model, --max-cost, --from-sprint (each value-taking), --dry-run (flag), positional USER_PROMPT, unknown -* exits 1. .ps1 adds --help/-h (required by sprint-03 AC #8). Both reject non-flag-prefixed extra positional? Both ASSIGN every non '-' token to USER_PROMPT (last write wins) -- identical behavior.

criteriaResults.description: --help exits 0 and prints usage
criteriaResults.evidence: Ran 'powershell -NoProfile -File harness/orchestrate.ps1 --help'. Printed full usage block, exit code 0.

criteriaResults.description: -h exits 0 and prints usage
criteriaResults.evidence: Ran 'powershell -NoProfile -File harness/orchestrate.ps1 -h'. Printed full usage block, exit code 0.

criteriaResults.description: No-arg invocation exits non-zero
criteriaResults.evidence: Ran with no arguments. Read-CliArgs validation at line 185-188 detected empty USER_PROMPT in mode 'new' and called exit 1. EXIT=1, error message printed. Matches orchestrate.sh:131-134.

criteriaResults.description: Unknown flag exits non-zero
criteriaResults.evidence: Ran with --bogus-flag. The default branch in Read-CliArgs (line 173-177) checked StartsWith('-'), called Write-LogError 'Unknown option: --bogus-flag', exit 1. Matches orchestrate.sh:119-122.

criteriaResults.description: Each .sh mode has a corresponding .ps1 entry point
criteriaResults.evidence: Mode dispatch table: new->Invoke-NewBuild (orchestrate.ps1:737), extend->Invoke-Extend (738), fix->Invoke-Fix (739), refactor->Invoke-Refactor (740), resume->Invoke-Resume (741), regression->Invoke-Regression (742). Mirrors orchestrate.sh:496-507. Every mode covered, default branch errors out.

criteriaResults.description: Same branch name format harness/sprint-NN
criteriaResults.evidence: orchestrate.ps1 calls New-SprintBranch and Merge-SprintBranch from git.ps1; sprint branches are formed as ${HarnessBranch}/sprint-${padded} per git.ps1 helpers (verified in sprint-01).

criteriaResults.description: Same tag names harness/plan, harness/sprint-NN/pass, harness/sprint-NN/attempt-K
criteriaResults.evidence: orchestrate.ps1:418 emits 'git tag harness/plan'. git.ps1:134 emits 'harness/sprint-${padded}/pass' inside Merge-SprintBranch. git.ps1:157 emits 'harness/sprint-${padded}/attempt-${Attempt}' inside Invoke-FailSprintAttempt. Matches orchestrate.sh:267 and git.sh:70/88.

criteriaResults.description: Same commit message templates
criteriaResults.evidence: orchestrate.ps1 calls Save-HarnessState with the exact strings: 'harness(contract): sprint-$padded agreed' (265), 'harness(eval): sprint-$padded PASS' (330), 'harness(eval): sprint-$padded FAILED' (344), 'harness(plan): product spec and sprint plan' (417). Save-HarnessState (git.ps1:169) just forwards the message to git commit -m. Sprint commit messages with [C-ID] are emitted by Invoke-Generator (sprint-02), not orchestrate.ps1, matching the .sh layering.

criteriaResults.description: Cost cap enforcement at the same points as the .sh
criteriaResults.evidence: orchestrate.sh calls check_cost_cap at three sites: line 278 (new build sprint loop), 348 (extend sprint loop), 473 (resume sprint loop). orchestrate.ps1 calls Test-CostCap at the parallel three sites: 430 (Invoke-NewBuild loop), 532 (Invoke-Extend loop), 701 (Invoke-Resume loop). Refactor and fix modes have no cost-cap check in either version. Caps wired from config: orchestrate.ps1:198-199 exports COST_CAP_PER_SPRINT and TOTAL_COST_CAP via Set-EnvDefaults so utils.ps1 helpers see them.

criteriaResults.description: Retry loop with maxSprintAttempts; on fail, attempt-K tag created and branch reset
criteriaResults.evidence: Invoke-SprintCycle (orchestrate.ps1:271-340) loops attempt 1..MaxSprintAttempts. Each iteration: New-SprintBranch (creates fresh branch off harness branch via git.ps1:83). On generator failure -> Invoke-FailSprintAttempt (tags attempt-K and stashes/deletes branch). On evaluator failure -> Invoke-FailSprintAttempt again. Mirrors orchestrate.sh:161-201. The branch reset is implicit because New-SprintBranch deletes any pre-existing branch with the same name (git.ps1:94).

criteriaResults.description: handoff.json updated between sprints via Update-Handoff
criteriaResults.evidence: orchestrate.ps1:327 calls Update-Handoff -SprintNumber -MergeSha -Tag -HarnessBranch on PASS. Helper lives in utils.ps1:316 (validated in sprint-02). Mirrors orchestrate.sh:188.

criteriaResults.description: Final PR creation tries gh pr create; on absence/failure writes harness-state/pr-body.md
criteriaResults.evidence: Invoke-NewBuild:451 calls New-HarnessPR (gh pr create). Lines 454-456 also call Test-CommandAvailable -Name 'gh' and write pr-body.md when missing. Same fallback in Invoke-Extend:539-541 and Invoke-Resume:710-712. Note: this fallback is an .ps1 addition over the .sh, but it is required by sprint-03 acceptance criterion #7 ('exits 0 after either creating a PR (if gh available) or writing harness-state/pr-body.md').

criteriaResults.description: harness/orchestrate.sh unchanged
criteriaResults.evidence: git diff main -- harness/orchestrate.sh produced empty output.

criteriaResults.description: No new .ps1 files in harness/hooks/
criteriaResults.evidence: ls harness/hooks/ shows only the four original .sh files: on-evaluator-stop.sh, on-generator-stop.sh, on-stop.sh, validate-schema.sh. No .ps1 added.

criteriaResults.description: Smoke: dot-source all seven libs in one PowerShell process
criteriaResults.evidence: Ran a script that dot-sourced utils, invoke, git, planner, contract, generator, evaluator in order; SMOKE_OK printed, exit 0. Sprint-01 and sprint-02 dot-source compatibility holds.

regressionResults.notes: All seven libs continue to dot-source in a single process; orchestrate.ps1 consumes the helpers (Save-HarnessState, Test-CostCap, Update-Handoff, Update-RegressionRegistry, Update-Progress) without redefinition. Forbidden-tool scan over orchestrate.ps1 returned zero hits.

orchestrate.ps1 calls Invoke-Planner, Invoke-ContractNegotiation, Invoke-Generator, Invoke-Evaluator, Invoke-Regression, Build-PRBody, New-HarnessPR, New-FixPR, New-HarnessIssue from the agent libs without re-implementing them. Smoke import check still passes.

summary: PASS. orchestrate.ps1 is a faithful port of orchestrate.sh:521 lines with no behavioral drift on any of the spot-checked surfaces. Argument parsing has 1:1 flag coverage with --help/-h added per the sprint contract; mode dispatch covers all six .sh modes; cost-cap enforcement points match exactly; tag, branch, and commit message formats are produced by the same git.ps1 helpers from sprint-01 (no orchestrator-emitted strings drift). Forbidden-tool AST scan is clean. orchestrate.sh is unchanged. The pr-body.md fallback when gh is missing is a contract-mandated addition over the .sh and is implemented in NewBuild, Extend, and Resume modes. No blockers.
